systems_thinking.interaction_description: Demand forecasting and the role of forecasting have a bidirectional relationship across their domains: decisions in demand forecasting directly influence how the role of forecasting is configured and managed, while feedback from the role of forecasting operations informs adjustments to demand forecasting strategy.
systems_thinking.explanation: The cross-section relationship between demand forecasting (Section A) and the role of forecasting (Section B) illustrates how supply chain concepts rarely exist in isolation. Demand forecasting provides strategic direction that shapes the role of forecasting decisions, while the role of forecasting generates operational data and constraints that feed back into demand forecasting planning. This bidirectional interaction means that changes in either domain can ripple across section boundaries, requiring managers to think holistically rather than in functional silos.
